source.publication: Benchmark Minerals / Crux Investor / ING Think

description: Multiple simultaneous copper supply disruptions converge to produce the largest copper market deficit in at least a decade. (1) Grasberg (FCX, Indonesia): force majeure extended — full restart now delayed from 2027 to 2028. World's second-largest copper mine operating at ~85% capacity until at least late 2027. Annual output cut: ~300M lbs (9% of FCX guidance). (2) Zambia: Vedanta copper smelter shutdown for maintenance window June-September 2026, removing processing capacity during a period of tight supply. (3) Chile: copper output -9.04% YoY (March 2026). (4) DRC: Kamoa-Kakula processing limited by sulfuric acid shortage. Cumulative impact: Morgan Stanley projects 600,000-tonne deficit in 2026. JPMorgan: 330,000T. ICSG: 150,000T. All three banks expect deficit conditions. At current LME price of $13,965/tonne, the market is partially pricing the tightness but analyst models have not fully incorporated the Grasberg-to-2028 extension.
user_notes: Múltiples disrupciones simultáneas que convierten el déficit de cobre de 2026 en un evento estructural, no cíclico. La extensión de Grasberg a 2028 es el hecho más relevante para la thesis — refuerza el argumento de supply inelástico. Este evento CONFIRMA struct_copper_datacenter_demand (lado de la oferta) y struct_energy_transition_grid. El 50% priced_in refleja que el precio ya ha subido a $13,965/T pero los modelos de analistas todavía no incorporan el déficit de 600k T. La extensión de Grasberg a 2028 fue parcialmente sorpresa (mercado esperaba 2027). Impacta directamente la thesis copper_miners — refuerza la tesis de supply tightness en la columna de supply constraints.
